skill.name: pagerduty-automation
skill.description: Automate PagerDuty tasks via Rube MCP (Composio): manage incidents, services, schedules, escalation policies, and on-call rotations. Always search tools first for current schemas.
skill.summary: Automate PagerDuty tasks via Rube MCP (Composio): manage incidents, services, schedules, escalation ...
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All 112 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES. The SKILL.md file is pure documentation describing PagerDuty automation via Rube MCP server. Static analyzer incorrectly flagged tool names (e.g., PAGERDUTY_FETCH_INCIDENT_LIST) as shell commands, but these are MCP tool identifiers in documentation text only. The hardcoded URL is the legitimate Rube MCP endpoint. No executable code, command injection vectors, or malicious patterns exist.

risk_factor_evidence.reasoning: Static scanner misidentified MCP tool names like PAGERDUTY_FETCH_INCIDENT_LIST as shell commands. These are documentation references to tool slugs, not executable code.
The URL https://rube.app/mcp is the documented MCP server endpoint users must add to their configuration. This is legitimate infrastructure documentation, not malicious exfiltration.
No cryptographic algorithms detected. Scanner flagged text patterns like 'type: service_reference' and status transitions which are not cryptographic operations.

content.value_statement: Manage PagerDuty incidents, services, schedules, and on-call rotations directly from Claude. Streamline incident response workflows without leaving your AI assistant.
actual_capabilities: Create, acknowledge, resolve, and snooze PagerDuty incidents
List and manage PagerDuty services and integrations
View and configure on-call schedules and rotations
Fetch incident analytics and alert details
Manage teams and business services in PagerDuty
limitations: Requires active PagerDuty account and Rube MCP connection
Cannot bypass PagerDuty rate limits - requires exponential backoff
Some operations require pre-existing resources (escalation policies for services)

use_cases.title: Incident Responder
use_cases.description: Quickly acknowledge or resolve incidents during on-call shifts by running natural language commands like 'Acknowledge incident P123ABC'

use_cases.title: DevOps Automation Engineer
use_cases.description: Programmatically create incidents, manage service configurations, and set up on-call schedules through Claude Code

use_cases.title: Team Lead
use_cases.description: View who is currently on-call across all schedules and create or modify escalation policies without logging into PagerDuty

prompt_templates.title: Check Current On-Call
prompt_templates.prompt: Who is currently on-call for the production support schedule?

prompt_templates.title: Acknowledge Incident
prompt_templates.prompt: Acknowledge incident P123ABC with note: 'Working on database connection issue'
prompt_templates.description: Acknowledge a specific incident with a note

prompt_templates.title: Create New Service
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new PagerDuty service named 'Checkout API' using escalation policy 'Default' with alert creation set to 'create_incidents'

prompt_templates.title: List Unacknowledged Incidents
prompt_templates.prompt: List all unacknowledged incidents from the last 2 hours, grouped by service

output_examples.input: Who is on-call for the platform team this week?
Create a high-urgency incident titled 'Payment Gateway Timeout' for the checkout service

best_practices: Always call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first to get current available tool schemas before running commands
Verify connection status shows ACTIVE before executing workflows
Use the preview tool before saving complex schedule changes
anti_patterns: Attempting to create incidents without verifying the PagerDuty connection is active
Trying to create a service without an existing escalation policy
Attempting to transition incidents backward (resolved -> triggered)

faq.question: Do I need a PagerDuty API key?
faq.answer: No API key needed. The skill uses Rube MCP which handles authentication through OAuth when you connect your PagerDuty account.

faq.question: Can I create incidents without a service?
faq.answer: No. Incident creation requires a service with both id and type: 'service_reference'. You must create or reference an existing service first.

faq.question: How do I check if my PagerDuty connection is working?
faq.answer: Run RUBE_MANAGE_CONNECTIONS with toolkit 'pagerduty' and verify the status shows ACTIVE before running any workflows.

faq.question: Can I transition an incident from resolved back to triggered?
faq.answer: No. PagerDuty incident status transitions are one-way: triggered -> acknowledged -> resolved. You cannot go backward.

faq.question: What happens if I hit rate limits?
faq.answer: PagerDuty enforces rate limits per account. Implement exponential backoff when receiving 429 responses and space out bulk operations.

faq.question: Can this skill manage business services and technical services?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill supports both technical services (via PAGERDUTY_CREATE_NEW_SERVICE) and business services (via PAGERDUTY_CREATE_BUSINESS_SERVICE).
